topics: Structure of a Simple Java Program
Java Programming Environment and Runtime Environment (Command Line & IDE)
Primitive Data Types and Wrapper Types
Operators - Arithmetic, Bitwise, Relational, Boolean Logical, Assignment, Conditional (Ternary)
Control Statements - Selection Statements, Iteration Statements and Jump Statements
OOP Concepts - Data Abstraction, Encapsulation, Inheritance, Polymorphism
Object-Oriented Programming in Java - Declaring Objects
Developing Database Applications using JDBC - JDBC Overview
SQL Fundamentals - Creating and Executing Basic SQL Queries

Project Suggestion: Library Management System using OOP and SQL - Design and implement the Circulation function in a Library Management System using Object-Oriented Programming principles in Java and limited use of SQL. Class Design: Book (title, author, ISBN, genre, status), User (user ID, name, contact information, borrowed books list), Library (lists of books and users), Librarian (inherits from User, adds/removes books, manages users), BorrowTransaction (transaction ID, book, user, borrow date, return date). Functionalities: Book Management (add, remove, update, search by title/author/ISBN/genre), User Management (register, search by user ID/name), Borrowing and Returning (check availability, update status). Deliverables: Design Document, Source Code, User Manual, Test Cases.

Project Suggestion: Online Payment Processing System using OOP and Dynamic Polymorphism - Design and implement an Online Payment Processing System using Object-Oriented Programming principles in Java with a focus on dynamic polymorphism. Class Design: Payment (abstract base class), CreditCardPayment, PayPalPayment, BankTransferPayment (inherit from Payment), PaymentProcessor (manages and processes different payment types). Functionalities: Add Payment Method, Process Payment (demonstrate dynamic polymorphism). Deliverables: Design Document, Source Code, User Manual, Test Cases.
